modules.name: MK_assets

Also provides GameEngine.SourceAssetRegistry.v1 source asset registry validation, deterministic text IO, Asset Identity v2 projection, and texture/mesh/audio/material/scene import metadata projection without importer execution, cooked artifact writes, .geindex package updates, renderer/RHI residency, or package streaming. staging with safe-point commits, recook import plan extraction, cooked package index serialization with content hashes, dependency edges, and deterministic recook decisions including ui_atlas package records and ui_atlas_texture dependency edges, canonical cooked GameEngine.UiAtlas.v1 metadata validation/serialization through UiAtlasMetadataDocument and UiAtlasMetadataGlyph without source image decoding, real font loading/rasterization, text shaping, renderer texture upload, or production atlas packing, deterministic cooked GameEngine.Tilemap.v1 metadata validation/serialization through TilemapMetadataDocument with AssetKind::tilemap and tilemap_texture dependency rows while keeping source decoding, production atlas packing, and native GPU sprite batching unsupported, shader source metadata, generated shader artifact tracking, shader descriptor reflection rows, shader compiler command planning including DXC Vulkan SPIR-V commands with -spirv and -fspv-target-env=vulkan1.3, shell-free shader tool runner validation, compiler output capture contracts, shader include dependency discovery, shader include dependency graph edge generation, deterministic shader artifact manifest serialization including reflection rows, texture/mesh/audio/material/scene import metadata, scene mesh/material/sprite dependency edge kinds, first-party texture/mesh/audio source document decoding including optional deterministic hex byte payloads, GameEngine.MorphMeshCpuSource.v1 morph mesh CPU source document serialization and validation with paired GameEngine.CookedMorphMeshCpu.v1 cook field emission, GameEngine.AnimationFloatClipSource.v1 animation float clip source document serialization and validation with paired GameEngine.CookedAnimationFloatClip.v1 cook field emission through the asset import tool, GameEngine.AnimationQuaternionClipSource.v1 3D TRS quaternion clip source document serialization and validation with paired GameEngine.CookedAnimationQuaternionClip.v1 cook field emission, AssetKind::animation_quaternion_clip, and animation_quaternion_clip import metadata planning, GameEngine.AnimationTransformBindingSource.v1 source document validation and deterministic text IO through AnimationTransformBindingSourceDocument, serialize_animation_transform_binding_source_document, and deserialize_animation_transform_binding_source_document for curve-target to named transform component authoring without scene-node resolution or runtime payload cooking, deterministic asset import planning, material definition serialization, GameEngine.MaterialGraph.v1 graph-authoring IR with deterministic text IO, validation diagnostics, and lowering to MaterialDefinition for the minimal v1 node set (graph_output, constant factors, texture nodes) without shader graph generation, GameEngine.MaterialGraphShaderExport.v0 bridge text IO plus deterministic HLSL stub emission for reviewed DXC planning without automatic package cook of export documents, material instance serialization/composition, fixed material factor uniform sizing, and explicit backend-neutral material binding metadata with uniform-buffer, sampled-texture, and Asset Identity v2 is implemented foundation-only, and Source Asset Registry v1 does not complete scene/render/UI/gameplay reference cleanup, renderer/RHI residency, package streaming, or 2D/3D playable vertical slices. External PNG decoded RGBA8 payloads, glTF position-only and interleaved position+normal+uv+tangent mesh payload conversion, common-audio decoded PCM payloads, and first-party GameEngine.Scene.v1 source validation/canonicalization are owned by MK_tools adapters so runtime/game code remains cooked-artifact-only.

modules.name: MK_ai
modules.purpose: Standard-library-only gameplay decision-flow foundation. Behavior Tree Foundation v0 provides value-type BehaviorTreeDesc, BehaviorTreeNodeDesc, BehaviorTreeLeafResult, BehaviorTreeTickResult, diagnostics, and visited-node traces for deterministic memoryless sequence, selector, action, and condition evaluation. Behavior Tree Blackboard Conditions v0 adds typed BehaviorTreeBlackboardValue rows for bool, signed integer, finite double, and string facts, deterministic BehaviorTreeBlackboard snapshots, BehaviorTreeBlackboardCondition bindings, and BehaviorTreeEvaluationContext so condition nodes can read caller-supplied blackboard snapshots while action nodes still use game-supplied leaf statuses. AI Perception Services v1 adds AiPerceptionAgent2D, AiPerceptionTarget2D, AiPerceptionSnapshot2D, build_ai_perception_snapshot_2d, and write_ai_perception_blackboard for explicit caller-supplied 2D sight/hearing classification, stable primary-target selection, duplicate target rejection, finite validation, and blackboard projection into caller-selected fact keys. It reports missing-root, duplicate-node-id, missing-child, empty-composite, leaf-has-children, missing-leaf-result, invalid-leaf-result, duplicate-leaf-result, duplicate-blackboard-key, invalid-blackboard-key, invalid-blackboard-value, duplicate-blackboard-condition, invalid-blackboard-condition, missing-blackboard-key, blackboard-type-mismatch, invalid-blackboard-operator, invalid-agent, invalid-target, duplicate-target-id, invalid-perception-blackboard-key, calculation-overflow, cycle, and max-depth diagnostics without owning async tasks, decorators/services, scene state, physics queries, navigation requests, persistent blackboard services, editor graph authoring, platform APIs, middleware, scripting, or third-party dependencies.

modules.name: MK_audio
modules.purpose: Device-independent audio foundations. Currently provides an AudioMixer that validates audio buses, voices, spatial listener/source descriptors, clip metadata, device render requests, resampling quality, and interleaved float clip sample data; tracks logical clip asset ids; produces deterministic mix commands; builds spatial voice/listener metadata for distance attenuation and stereo pan; plans sample-frame voice scheduling; records streaming buffer availability, device format conversion intent, and underrun diagnostics; provides AudioStreamingQueue for decoded chunk append/consume/starvation state without codecs; renders deterministic interleaved float PCM buffers with gain, selectable nearest/linear resampling, channel conversion, and clamping; exposes AudioDeviceStreamRequest, AudioDeviceStreamPlan, plan_audio_device_stream, and render_audio_device_stream_interleaved_float so host adapters can fill output queues from AudioMixer PCM using bounded render chunks; and does not open OS audio devices.

modules.name: MK_audio_sdl3
modules.purpose: Optional SDL3 desktop audio adapter. Currently provides SdlAudioRuntime for SDL_INIT_AUDIO lifetime and SdlAudioDevice for default playback streams through SDL_OpenAudioDeviceStream, pause/resume/clear controls, queued-frame reporting, silence queuing, and interleaved float PCM queuing while keeping SDL stream and device handles private.

modules.name: MK_math
modules.purpose: Standard-library-only 2D/3D math primitives for games and engine systems, including vectors, matrices, unit quaternions, quaternion vector rotation, Mat4::rotation_quat, and transforms with position, scale, and radians-based rotation.

modules.purpose: Standard-library-only deterministic engine core. modules.name: MK_ui_renderer
modules.purpose: First-party runtime UI renderer adapter that consumes MK_ui RendererSubmission payloads through the existing MK_renderer public API without making MK_ui depend on renderer code. Currently provides UiRendererTheme token-to-color mapping, resolves styled UI boxes to renderer colors, converts RendererBox bounds into SpriteCommand rectangles, resolves deterministic MonospaceTextLayoutPolicy glyph rows through UiRendererGlyphAtlasPalette and UiRendererGlyphAtlasBinding by font_family plus glyph id into atlas-backed text glyph sprite submissions, resolves ImageAdapterPayload rows through UiRendererImagePalette and UiRendererImageBinding into image sprite submissions with deterministic resource_id-first and asset_uri-fallback lookup, can attach first-party cooked atlas page and UV metadata for glyph and image bindings, can build UiRendererImagePalette rows from MK_runtime RuntimeUiAtlasPayload package metadata for the native-ui-atlas-package-metadata proof, can build glyph palette rows from RuntimeUiAtlasGlyph package metadata through build_ui_renderer_glyph_atlas_palette_from_runtime_ui_atlas for the Runtime UI Glyph Atlas Package Bridge v1 proof, submits styled UI boxes, resolved text glyphs, and resolved image placeholders through IRenderer, reports text run, text adapter row, text glyph availability/resolution/missing/submission, image placeholder, image sprite submission, missing image resource, accessibility node, and adapter diagnostic availability, and leaves concrete text shaping/font loading/rasterization/glyph atlas generation/source image decoding/production atlas packing/renderer texture upload/OS accessibility bridges behind their separate MK_ui adapter boundaries.

Path Following / Steering Foundation v0 adds NavigationPoint2, NavigationPointPathBuildResult, build_navigation_point_path_result, build_navigation_point_path, NavigationPathFollowRequest, advance_navigation_path_following, NavigationArriveSteeringRequest, and calculate_navigation_arrive_steering for grid-path-to-point conversion, explicit path-follow cursor advancement, max-step/arrival-radius movement, remaining-distance reporting, and single-agent arrive steering. Navigation Agent Controller Foundation v0 adds NavigationAgentConfig, NavigationAgentState, NavigationAgentUpdateRequest, NavigationAgentUpdateResult, make_navigation_agent_state, replace_navigation_agent_path, cancel_navigation_agent_move, and update_navigation_agent for value-type idle/moving/reached/cancelled/invalid movement state, speed/delta clamping, final-destination slowing, path replacement, and desired velocity from actual path movement. These foundations do not migrate native backend destruction, implement vendor-specialized async GPU upload queues or streaming package integration, a GPU allocator, residency budgets, package streaming, GPU markers beyond the scoped API, editor resource management/capture tooling execution beyond reviewed request handoff, production renderer scheduling, or 2D/3D playable vertical-slice readiness. The D3D12 IRhiDevice bridge implements private DSV-backed public depth attachments, native depth pipeline state, active pass/pipeline color-depth format validation, readback-observable depth-test proof, sampled-depth proof, depth-aware postprocess proof, and directional shadow receiver proof; the Vulkan bridge implements private depth-aspect image views for sampled descriptors, depth|stencil D24S8 barrier aspect masks, required synchronization2 feature query/device enablement, depth_write synchronization2 barriers, dynamic-rendering depth attachments, native depth pipeline state, active pass/pipeline color-depth format validation, environment-gated depth-ordered readback proof, environment-gated visible sampled-depth readback proof, environment-gated postprocess-depth readback proof, and environment-gated directional shadow receiver readback proof while keeping Vulkan handles private.
